rag_rules: Preserve the existing AADL architecture and insert any generated annex content only within a valid owner scope.
Keep component type contracts with feature-referenced assumptions or guarantees in type scope; keep implementation-level bindings in implementation scope.
Use the input AADL model as the authority for valid owners, features, subcomponents, connections, and existing annex locations.
generic_rules: Do not move, rename, or alter structural declarations when adding annex content.
Maintain scope discipline: type scope may reference visible component features, while implementation scope may depend on implementation structure.
If inserted content would require symbols not valid in the chosen owner scope, defer rather than forcing cross-scope references.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE content is wrapped in an `annex agree {** ... **};` block.
Implementation-scoped content should appear in a valid implementation owner and not before the structural elements it depends on.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-only paths or subcomponent references into a component type owner.
Do not treat AADL property names or unit tokens as in-scope AGREE variables during fusion.
